item.title: 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop
item.description: NERC, in collaboration with OE and ORNL, will host the 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop on Aug. 18-21, 2026, in Washington, D.C. Researchers, consultants, utilities, system operators, and reliability organizations will explore advancements in EMT simulations, including modeling, simulation techniques, input data structure, and automation tools to support evolving industry needs. This workshop will also feature hands-on tutorials on EMT simulation that is supported by DOE i2x.

item.title: 3 practical steps to ensure the US industrial boom runs on electricity
item.description: Long-term electricity procurement, on-site generation, efficiency and flexibility can give manufacturers more ways to manage their energy bills, writes the World Wildlife Fund’s Cihang Yuan.

item.title: Best Buy adds more solar generation to portfolio
item.description: The new solar sites include the retailer’s first store with a rooftop “community solar garden” as it looks to a 2040 net-zero target.

item.title: Data centers drove $6.3B in PJM capacity auction costs: market monitor
item.description: In the PJM’s last four capacity auctions, data center-related capacity charges were nearly half of the $63.6 billion in total charges, Monitoring Analytics says.

item.title: We don’t need more proof that flexibility works. We need more people using it.
item.description: "Flexibility works. The real bottleneck is scaling participation fast enough to matter."

item.title: AI data center growth could force US utilities to rethink generation plans, BofA says
item.description: Data center demand will outpace planned utility capacity additions by more than 100 GW through 2030, increasing reliance on on-site gas generation and battery storage, Bank of America analysts project.

item.title: PJM capacity auction results compound ‘alarm bells’: FERC Chairman Swett
item.description: At its monthly meeting, FERC set data center reliability standard deadlines, ordered CAISO and SPP to report on “seams” issues and advanced a complaint over cost recovery for a New Jersey power line.

item.title: Renewables remain cheapest, but their LCOE is rising: Lazard
item.description: Utility-scale solar’s levelized cost of electricity ranges between $40/MWh and $98/MWh, while combined cycle gas ranges between $51/MWh and $129/MWh, said a report from Lazard.

item.title: Google inks deal for massive Arkansas solar and storage project
item.description: With a target completion date of 2029, the project is expected to deliver 2.5 GW of generation and 2.9 GWh of battery storage to the regional grid, the tech giant said.

item.title: What data center developers need to know about FERC’s large load directives
item.description: The June 18 show cause orders finally take aim at the load interconnection mess. But the customers who get power faster will be the ones who show up ready to flex, writes Shalin Savalia, a senior electrical engineer.

item.title: Indiana regulators investigate utility ROEs, ‘trackers’ in affordability review
item.description: AES, American Electric Power, CenterPoint Energy, Duke Energy and NiSource could be affected under a “more consumer-oriented utility commission policy,” Paul Patterson, a Glenrock Associates equity analyst, told Utility Dive.
